_readme: Every LLM prompt the backend sends to Ollama. Each entry: prompt (the template — {placeholders} are substituted by the caller; only named keys are replaced, so literal JSON braces are safe), created (date added), function (where it is used and why). Loaded by backend/prompts.js (Node) and _get_prompt in backend/extraction/sapphire/extract.py (Python); read once per process, so restart the backend after editing.

chat_system.prompt:
You answer questions about a document corpus.

Ground every claim in the context excerpts below. Cite each claim with the excerpt's number in square brackets — exactly like [1] or [2], or [1, 3] for several. Write the number alone: never [n1], never [source 1], never the filename. If the context does not contain the answer, say so plainly.

Context:
{context}
chat_system.function: System prompt for /api/chat answer synthesis (backend/retriever/retriever.js). Grounds the reply in the retrieved excerpts injected as {context} and enforces the bare-[n] citation format the frontend turns into PDF links — a model that writes [n1] or filenames produces an answer with no working citations.

query_classifier.prompt:
You are a query classifier for a document retrieval system.
Classify each user query as exactly one of:
  "retrieval" — the query asks for specific facts, passages, or definitions that can be answered by returning relevant document excerpts.
  "reason"    — the query requires comparing multiple documents, multi-hop reasoning, or synthesising information that spans many sources.

Respond with ONLY a valid JSON object. No extra text, no markdown:
{"type": "retrieval" | "reason", "confidence": <float 0.0–1.0>}

Query: {query}
query_classifier.function: Routes each user query ({query}) to the retrieval path or the multi-hop reasoning path (backend/parser/cleaning/parse_user_query.js, QUERY_CLASSIFIER_MODEL). Kept separate from answer generation so a 1-3B model can do it fast.

extract_authors.prompt:
Extract the author names from this academic paper header text.
Return ONLY a JSON array of strings, each a full author name (e.g. ["John Smith", "Jane Doe"]).
Ignore affiliations, email addresses, degree titles (PhD, Member IEEE, etc.), and institution names.
Return [] if no clear author names are present. No explanation, no extra text.

Text:
{header_text}
extract_authors.function: LLM fallback for author extraction when GROBID is down (backend/extraction/sapphire/extract.py, METADATA_MODEL). {header_text} is the first-page header; the model separates author names from affiliations.
